name: Interpretation Forecast review for Proteasome Enhancement bundle
summary: The proposed outlook is appropriately conservative for a first public baseline: it assigns low-confidence animal_signal because organismal support is repeated but worm-heavy, keeps human translation below human_biomarker_signal because human evidence is cell-based, and uses rating-change criteria that require human pathway engagement or functional benefit before any upgrade.

findings.claim_or_issue: The outlook assigns animal_signal even though the organismal lifespan and proteostasis evidence is dominated by C. elegans rather than mammalian aging studies.
findings.why_it_matters: The stage should not imply broad mammalian or human validation.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; the outlook labels the evidence as worm-heavy, keeps confidence low, and requires mammalian aging or human pathway-engagement data for upgrades.

findings.claim_or_issue: Human-relevant support consists of fibroblast, mammalian-cell, HEK293, and iPSC-derived motor neuron evidence rather than an intervention study in humans.
findings.why_it_matters: Calling this human_biomarker_signal would overstate the directness of the evidence.
findings.recommended_action: Retain animal_signal and the support-map section that explicitly limits human translation to cell-based pathway plausibility.

findings.claim_or_issue: The support map spans Nrf2-linked 18alpha-GA, RPN-6, cAMP-PKA/Rpn6 phosphorylation, rolipram, PA28gamma/PSME-3, and open-gate proteasome biology.
findings.why_it_matters: Mechanistic heterogeneity can make a track look more intervention-ready than it is if treated as one coherent therapy class.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; the staged blockers, confidence rationale, and rating-change criteria avoid a near-term LEV window and require selective pathway engagement plus safety data.
